additionalVolumes.description: Provides configuration options to mount additional volumes to specific directories in the HiveMQ container as a list.

containerName.description: The optional name of the container to which the volume is mounted. The default container name is `hivemq`.

csi.description: Configures the Container Storage Interface (CSI) volume source. Required for type `csi`.

driver.description: The name of the CSI driver to use (e.g. `secrets-store.csi.k8s.io`).

fsType.description: The filesystem type to mount (e.g. `ext4`, `xfs`, `ntfs`). The value is passed through to the CSI driver and the supported types depend on the driver and the host operating system.

nodePublishSecretRef.description: The name of the Kubernetes Secret to pass to the CSI driver for `NodePublishVolume`.

readOnly.description: Mounts the CSI volume as read-only.

volumeAttributes.description: Driver-specific key-value attributes passed through to the CSI driver.

mountName.description: Defines the name of the volume mount to be used for the StatefulSet spec. For type `sharedPersistentVolumeClaim`, the `mountName` (or `name`, if not set) must match a `volumeClaimTemplates` entry.

name.description: The name of the Kubernetes resource backing the volume. Required for types `configMap`, `secret`, `persistentVolumeClaim` and `sharedPersistentVolumeClaim`. For `persistentVolumeClaim` and `sharedPersistentVolumeClaim`, this entry is used as the PersistentVolumeClaim name. For types `csi`, `emptyDir` and `projected`, `name` is used as the volume identifier only when `mountName` is not set (prefer setting `mountName`).

path.description: Configures the path of the directory to which the volume is mounted in the container. If the directory already exists then the contents are overwritten!

projectedSources.description: Configures sources for additional volumes of type `projected`. This entry must contain a list of the volume source configurations that the Kubernetes Projected Volumes support.

subPath.description: Optional name for a subPath for a file to be mounted. The generated volume mount path is the concatenation of the `path` and `subPath` values. If a volume is mounted with subPath, the contents of the directory are not overwritten. However, changes to a ConfigMap that are mounted via subPath are not propagated to the container. In such cases, a pod restart is required to apply changes. Not applicable for the `sharedPersistentVolumeClaim` type.

hivemqFolders.description: Redirects specific HiveMQ platform directories into named subfolders within the same PersistentVolumeClaim mount path. Each key selects a HiveMQ directory type and each value defines the subfolder name where that directory content is stored. The chart automatically sets the corresponding environment variable for each configured key.

audit.description: Configures the subfolder name for the HiveMQ audit log directory. Sets the `HIVEMQ_AUDIT_FOLDER` environment variable.

backup.description: Configures the subfolder name for the HiveMQ backup directory. Sets the `HIVEMQ_BACKUP_FOLDER` environment variable.

data.description: Configures the subfolder name for the HiveMQ data directory. Sets the `HIVEMQ_DATA_FOLDER` environment variable.

heapDump.description: Configures the subfolder name for the HiveMQ heap dump directory. Sets the `HIVEMQ_HEAPDUMP_FOLDER` environment variable.

log.description: Configures the subfolder name for the HiveMQ log directory. Sets the `HIVEMQ_LOG_FOLDER` environment variable.

type.description: Defines the type of volume to be mounted.

config.description: Defines how the HiveMQ Platform configuration is deployed as a Kubernetes object.

annotations.description: Defines specific annotations to be applied to the Kubernetes ConfigMap or Kubernetes Secret that contains the HiveMQ Platform configuration.

create.description: Boolean value, that configures whether to use a default HiveMQ configuration.

createAs.description: Defines how the HiveMQ Platform configuration should be stored into. Either a Kubernetes ConfigMap or a Kubernetes Secret.

customLogbackConfig.description: Optional setting to provide a custom HiveMQ Platform logging configuration (logback.xml) from a file using --set-file config.customLogbackConfig=your-logback-config.xml.

customTracingConfig.description: Optional setting to provide a custom HiveMQ Platform tracing configuration (tracing.xml) from a file using --set-file config.customTracingConfig=your-tracing-config.xml.

dataHub.description: Optional setting to enable HiveMQ Data Hub functionality.

behaviorValidationEnabled.description: Optional setting to enable behavior validation. The default setting is `false`.

dataValidationEnabled.description: Optional setting to enable data validation. The default setting is `false`.

includeDisabled.description: Defines whether all Data Hub options are rendered in HiveMQ Platform configuration, including features set to `false`. This allows explicitly disabling DataHub. The default setting is `false`.

scriptingEnabled.description: Optional setting to enable scripting functionality. The default setting is `false`.

labels.description: Defines specific labels to be applied to the Kubernetes ConfigMap or Kubernetes Secret that contains the HiveMQ Platform configuration.

name.description: The name of an existing Kubernetes ConfigMap or Secret with a valid HiveMQ configuration.

overrideHiveMQConfig.description: Optional setting to provide the HiveMQ configuration from a file using --set-file config.overrideHiveMQConfig=your-hivemq-config.xml.

overrideStatefulSet.description: Optional setting to provide the StatefulSetSpec configuration from a file using --set-file config.overrideStateFulSet=your-statefulsetspec.yml.

platformLicense.description: Configures the HiveMQ Platform license configuration (license.xml).

licenseKey.description: The platform license key identifier. When non-empty, a license.xml is generated and included in the configuration.

overrideLicenseConfig.description: Optional setting to provide a custom license.xml from a file using --set-file config.platformLicense.overrideLicenseConfig=your-license.xml.

controlCenter.description: Optional setting to override the default username and password for the HiveMQ Control Center when the default configuration is used.

credentialsSecret.description: Configures the Control Center credentials by using an existing Kubernetes Secret.

name.description: Configures the name of an existing Kubernetes Secret containing the Control Center credentials.

passwordKey.description: Configures the key for the Kubernetes Secret used holding the Control Center password.

usernameKey.description: Configures the key for the Kubernetes Secret used holding the Control Center username.

password.description: The SHA256 encoded password for the HiveMQ Control Center user.

username.description: The name of the HiveMQ Control Center user.

configMapName.description: Configures the name of the Kubernetes ConfigMap that contains the extension configuration.

customizationUri.description: The HTTP URL from which to load the extension customization.

enabled.description: Boolean value that defines whether the extension is enabled.

extensionUri.description: The location from which to load the extension. Possible values are: preinstalled or an HTTP URL.

name.description: The defined name of the extension from the extensions.xml file.

requestHeaderSecretName.description: Configures the name of the Kubernetes Secret that contains headers for HTTP requests for custom extension and extension customization downloads.

secretName.description: Configures the name of the Kubernetes Secret that contains the extension configuration.

skipHttpsCertificateValidation.description: Boolean value to skip certificate validation when downloading a custom extension and extension customizations via the HTTPS protocol.

skipHttpsHostnameVerification.description: Boolean value to skip hostname verification when downloading a custom extension and extension customizations via the HTTPS protocol.

startMandatory.description: Determines how the HiveMQ broker behaves if the startup of the extension fails.

startPriority.description: The start priority of the extension.

supportsHotReload.description: Boolean value that specifies whether the extension configuration is hot-reloadable. The extension must explicitly support hot-reload.

extensionMap.description: Map-based extension configuration. Keys are extension names. Entries override matching extensions by name. Extensions defined only in extensionMap are added as new entries. Supports hierarchical value file overrides.

configMapName.description: Configures the name of the Kubernetes ConfigMap that contains the extension configuration.

customizationUri.description: The HTTP URL from which to load the extension customization.

enabled.description: Boolean value that defines whether the extension is enabled.

extensionUri.description: The location from which to load the extension. Possible values are: preinstalled or an HTTP URL.

requestHeaderSecretName.description: Configures the name of the Kubernetes Secret that contains headers for HTTP requests for custom extension and extension customization downloads.

secretName.description: Configures the name of the Kubernetes Secret that contains the extension configuration.

skipHttpsCertificateValidation.description: Boolean value to skip certificate validation when downloading a custom extension and extension customizations via the HTTPS protocol.

skipHttpsHostnameVerification.description: Boolean value to skip hostname verification when downloading a custom extension and extension customizations via the HTTPS protocol.

startMandatory.description: Determines how the HiveMQ broker behaves if the startup of the extension fails.

startPriority.description: The start priority of the extension.

supportsHotReload.description: Boolean value that specifies whether the extension configuration is hot-reloadable. The extension must explicitly support hot-reload.

hivemqClientEventHistory.description: Configures HiveMQ Client Event History options.

hivemqClusterFailureDetection.description: Configures HiveMQ Cluster failure detection. See https://docs.hivemq.com/hivemq/latest/user-guide/cluster.html#failure-detection.

heartbeat.description: Continuously sends a heartbeat between nodes.

interval.description: The interval in which a heartbeat message is sent to other nodes.

timeout.description: Amount of time that is tolerated for the response to a heartbeat message before a node is temporarily removed from the cluster.

tcpHealthCheck.description: Holds an open TCP connection between nodes.

bindAddress.description: The network address to bind to.

bindPort.description: The port to bind to. 0 uses an ephemeral port.

externalAddress.description: The external address to bind to if the node is behind some kind of NAT.

externalPort.description: The external port to bind to if the node is behind some kind of NAT.

portRange.description: Port range to check on other nodes.

replicaCount.description: Number of replicas to be persisted across cluster nodes.

keepAliveAllowUnlimited.description: Defines whether clients can have unlimited keep alive.

keepAliveMax.description: Defines the maximum keep alive value that a client can set.

maxPacketSize.description: Defines the maximum packet size that the broker accepts from clients.

maxQualityOfService.description: Defines the maximum Quality of Service (QoS) level allowed in MQTT PUBLISH messages.

messageExpiryMaxInterval.description: Defines the maximum message expiry value that clients can set.

queuedMessagesMaxSize.description: Sets the maximum number of messages the HiveMQ broker can store per client.

queuedMessagesStrategy.description: Defines how messages that arrive at the HiveMQ broker when the message queue of the corresponding client is full are handled.

retainedMessages.description: Defines whether retained messages are supported.

serverReceiveMaximum.description: Defines the maximum number of concurrent publishes the broker accepts from one client.

sharedSubscriptions.description: Defines whether clients can use shared subscriptions.

sessionExpiryMaxInterval.description: Defines the maximum session expiry value that clients can set.

subscriptionIdentifier.description: Defines whether clients can use subscription identifiers to associate received publishes with their subscriptions.

topicAliasEnabled.description: Defines whether topic aliases are enabled by the broker.

topicAliasMaxPerClient.description: The number of topic aliases available per client.

wildcardSubscriptions.description: Defines whether clients can use wildcard characters in topic filters.

hivemqMqttAddons.description: Configures HiveMQ MQTT Add-on options. See https://docs.hivemq.com/hivemq/latest/user-guide/mqtt-add-ons.html.

deadMessagesTopic.description: Captures all dead messages on the HiveMQ system.

droppedMessagesTopic.description: Captures all dropped messages on the HiveMQ system.

expiredMessagesTopic.description: Captures all expired messages on the HiveMQ system.

hivemqMqttSecurity.description: Configures HiveMQ MQTT security options. See https://docs.hivemq.com/hivemq/latest/user-guide/configuration.html#security.

allowEmptyClientId.description: Allows the use of empty client ids. If this is set to true, HiveMQ automatically generates random client ids, when clientId in the CONNECT packet is empty.

allowRequestProblemInformation.description: Allows the client to request the problem information. When set to false, no reason string or user property values are sent to clients.

controlCenterAuditLog.description: If audit logging for the control center is enabled.

payloadFormatValidation.description: Enables the UTF-8 validation of UTF-8 PUBLISH payloads.

restApiAuditLog.description: Defines whether audit logging for the REST API is enabled.

utf8Validation.description: Enables the UTF-8 validation of topic names and client IDs.

hivemqOverloadProtection.description: Configures HiveMQ Cluster overload protection. See https://docs.hivemq.com/hivemq/latest/user-guide/overload-protection.html.

enabled.description: Enables the HiveMQ Cluster overload protection.

hivemqRestrictions.description: Configures the HiveMQ restriction options. See https://docs.hivemq.com/hivemq/latest/user-guide/restrictions.html.

incomingBandwidthThrottling.description: The maximum incoming traffic as bytes per second (b/s).

maxClientIdLength.description: The maximum number of characters HiveMQ accepts in an MQTT-client ID.

maxConnections.description: The maximum number of concurrent MQTT connections the HiveMQ instance allows.

maxTopicLength.description: The maximum number of characters HiveMQ accepts in a topic string.

noConnectIdleTimeout.description: The time in milliseconds that HiveMQ waits for a CONNECT message of a client before an open TCP socket is closed.

image.description: Configures the container image details for the HiveMQ Platform.

digest.description: Optional image digest for the HiveMQ Platform image. When set, the rendered image reference is `<repository>/<name>:<tag>@<digest>`, or `<repository>/<name>@<digest>` when `tag` is empty. Must be in the form `<algorithm>:<hex>`, e.g. `sha256:<64 hex chars>`.

name.description: The name of the HiveMQ Platform container image to be used.

pullPolicy.description: Defines the imagePullPolicy that Kubernetes uses to download the HiveMQ Platform container image. Possible values: `IfNotPresent`, `Never`, or `Always`. Default: `IfNotPresent`.

pullSecretName.description: Configures the name of the ImagePullSecret Kubernetes uses to authenticate against the selected repository. Applies to all containers in HiveMQ Platform pods including init and sidecar containers.

repository.description: Configures from which repository the HiveMQ Platform image is pulled.

tag.description: The identifier of the HiveMQ Platform container image variant to be pulled.

license.description: Defines all HiveMQ file based licenses to be used for the HiveMQ Platform.

additionalLicenses.description: Adds additional main HiveMQ file based licenses to the Kubernetes Secret as .lic files, if needed.

annotations.description: Defines specific annotations to be applied to the Kubernetes Secret that contains the HiveMQ file based licenses.

create.description: Creates a Kubernetes Secret for all configured HiveMQ file based licenses.

data.description: The main HiveMQ file based license as a string. By default, this data must be a Base64 encoded string. Otherwise, set isLicenseBase64Encoded=false to use a clear string.

dataHub.description: Configures a list of HiveMQ Data Hub licenses to include as part of the Kubernetes Secret.

extensions.description: Defines a list of HiveMQ Enterprise Extension licenses to include as part of the Kubernetes Secret.

labels.description: Defines specific labels to be applied to the Kubernetes Secret that contains the HiveMQ file based licenses.

name.description: Defines the name of the Kubernetes Secret that contains the HiveMQ file based licenses.

overrideLicense.description: Sets the main HiveMQ file based license with your own file from a specified license path.

isLicenseBase64Encoded.description: Configures whether to use a Base64 encoded string (data) or clear string (stringData) for the Kubernetes Secret. The default setting is `true`.

rollingRestart.description: Configures the rolling restart behavior for this HiveMQ Platform.

lastPodMinAgeSeconds.description: The minimum age in seconds that the last restarted Pod must have before the next Pod can be restarted during a rolling restart. When set, this overrides the global operator configuration. If not set, the global operator default of 30 seconds is used.

monitoredResources.description: Configures Kubernetes resources (ConfigMaps and Secrets) to monitor for changes. When one of the files in a monitored resource changes, the HiveMQ Platform pods will perform a rolling restart.

name.description: The name of the ConfigMap or Secret to monitor for changes.

type.description: The type of resource to monitor. Must be ConfigMap or Secret.

files.description: List of files to monitor within the resource. If empty or omitted, all files in the resource will be monitored.

monitoring.description: Enables the ServiceMonitor resource and Grafana dashboard creation for the platform.

enabled.description: Defines whether the monitoring configuration for the HiveMQ Platform is enabled. The default setting is `false`.

create.description: Boolean value that configures whether to use the default HiveMQ Platform Grafana dashboard ConfigMap. The default setting is `true`.

namespace.description: Namespace where to deploy the Grafana dashboard ConfigMap. Defaults to the namespace of the HiveMQ Platform.

overrideDashboard.description: Sets the Grafana dashboard content for the HiveMQ Platform Grafana dashboard ConfigMap with your own JSON file from a specified path.

interval.description: Defines how often Prometheus scrapes metrics from the operator endpoints. Valid formats include `1d`, `1h30m`, `5m`, and `10s`. Defaults to 15 seconds.

namespace.description: Namespace where to deploy the ServiceMonitor. Defaults to the namespace of the HiveMQ Platform.

releaseName.description: Helm chart release name of the Prometheus Monitoring stack available in the Kubernetes cluster. Defaults to the HiveMQ Platform Helm chart release name.

scrapeTimeout.description: Defines the maximum duration allowed for scraping metrics from the endpoints defined in the ServiceMonitor resource. Valid formats include `1d`, `1h30m`, `5m`, and `10s`. Defaults to 10 seconds.

containerSecurityContext.description: Configures container security for both HiveMQ Init and HiveMQ main container. Some fields are present in both SecurityContext and PodSecurityContext. When both are set, the values in SecurityContext take precedence.

persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy.description: Optional setting that defines the retention policy controlling the lifecycle of PersistentVolumeClaims created from `volumeClaimTemplates` for your HiveMQ Platform pods. Any valid `persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy` Kubernetes configuration can be used.

podSecurityContext.description: Configures pod-level security for the HiveMQ Platform pod. Some fields are also present in container.securityContext. Field values of container.securityContext take precedence over field values of PodSecurityContext.

operator.description: Provides the selection criteria to identify which operator manages the selected HiveMQ Platform custom resource.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the HiveMQ Platform custom resource.

labels.description: Labels to add to the HiveMQ Platform custom resource.

selector.description: Selector name to configure which HiveMQ Platform Operator manages this platform.

podDisruptionBudget.description: Configures a PodDisruptionBudget for the HiveMQ Platform pods to control voluntary disruptions such as node drains or cluster autoscaler scale-down events.

enabled.description: Defines whether the PodDisruptionBudget resource is created for the HiveMQ Platform pods. Requires either minAvailable or maxUnavailable (but not both) to also be set.

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum number of HiveMQ Platform pods that can be unavailable during voluntary disruptions. Accepts an integer or a percentage string (for example, `1` or `"25%"`). Mutually exclusive with `minAvailable`.

minAvailable.description: Minimum number of HiveMQ Platform pods that must remain available during voluntary disruptions. Accepts an integer or a percentage string (for example, `2` or `"50%"`). Mutually exclusive with `maxUnavailable`.

unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy.description: Controls how the PodDisruptionBudget treats unhealthy HiveMQ Platform pods. Requires Kubernetes 1.26 or newer. Leave empty to use the cluster default. `IfHealthyBudget` only allows eviction of unhealthy pods if the budget is satisfied (can deadlock if pods crashloop). `AlwaysAllow` always allows eviction of unhealthy pods.

podScheduling.description: Configures how the HiveMQ Platform pods are placed on your Kubernetes nodes.

affinity.description: Optional setting that defines the affinity configuration for your HiveMQ Platform pods. Any valid `affinity` Kubernetes configuration can be used.

nodeSelector.description: Optional setting that defines the node selector configuration for your HiveMQ Platform pods. Constrains the HiveMQ Platform pods to run on Kubernetes nodes with matching labels.

priorityClassName.description: Optional setting that defines the priority class name for your HiveMQ Platform pods. The referenced Kubernetes `PriorityClass` must already exist in the cluster. Protects HiveMQ Platform pods from being preempted during cluster events such as node upgrades or autoscaling.

tolerations.description: Optional setting that defines the pod tolerations configuration for your HiveMQ Platform pods.

pulse.description: Defines the HiveMQ Pulse configuration options.

annotations.description: Defines custom annotations for the Kubernetes Secret that contains the HiveMQ Pulse configuration.

create.description: Creates a Kubernetes Secret for the HiveMQ Pulse configuration.

data.description: Inlines the HiveMQ Pulse configuration as a string. By default, this data is expected as Base64 encoded string. Set "isPulseConfigBase64Encoded=false" to provide a plain string.

isPulseConfigBase64Encoded.description: Indicates whether the HiveMQ Pulse configuration is provided as Base64 encoded string (data) or plain content (stringData) for the rendered Kubernetes Secret. This is useful for third party tools that handle content through placeholders, such as the ArgoCD Vault plugin.

labels.description: Defines custom labels for the Kubernetes Secret that contains the HiveMQ Pulse configuration.

name.description: The name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the HiveMQ Pulse configuration. To create a new Kubernetes Secret, set create=true. Otherwise, false to reuse an existing Secret.

overridePulseConfig.description: Overrides the HiveMQ Pulse configuration via file using --set-file pulse.overridePulseConfig=your-pulse-config.xml.

tlsCipherSuites.description: Optional setting to explicitly define the accepted cipher suites.

externalTrafficPolicy.description: Describes if the Service routes external traffic to node-local or cluster-wide endpoints. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/networking/virtual-ips/#external-traffic-policy.

hivemqConnectOverloadProtection.description: Configures the connect overload protection for the HiveMQ MQTT and WebSocket listeners.

connectBurstSize.description:
The maximum amount of simultaneous `CONNECT` messages that the listener allows for short periods. The burst size cannot be set to 0 (zero). 
When no burst rate is specified, the listener allows a burst rate that is double the defined connect rate.

connectRate.description: The maximum sustained rate of connects that the listener allows per second. The connect rate cannot be set to 0 (zero). The default setting is `500`.

enabled.description: Defines whether connect overload protection is enabled. Possible values are `true` and `false`. The default setting is `true`.

hivemqListenerName.description: Defines the optional custom name for the MQTT, WebSocket and REST API listeners.

nodePort.description: Defines the port that the service exposes to external clients when the service type is set to `NodePort`.

path.description: The last part of the path to the WebSocket. For the HiveMQ WebSocket listener, /mqtt routes to ws://broker.hivemq.com:8000/mqtt.

sessionAffinity.description: Used to maintain session affinity. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#virtual-ips-and-service-proxies

type.description: Session affinity type. Must be ClientIP or None. Defaults to `ClientIP` for `control-services` service type.

tlsProtocols.description: Optional setting to explicitly define the accepted TLS version.

anonymousUsageStatistics.description: Optional setting for anonymous usage statistics.

enabled.description: Optional setting to disable usage statistics.
Defines whether Helm test pods should be created. When set to `false`, no test pods are rendered and a `helm test` command will not run any tests.
